steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows the title Home Ground: A Guide to the American ______ and is followed by 'by Barry Lopez and Debra Gwartney, the book celebrates...' — a new independent clause. The blank must close the first sentence and allow the second sentence to begin correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Landscape, edited: a comma before 'edited by Barry Lopez...' leaves both clauses joined only by a comma — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — Landscape and edited: 'and edited' creates an awkward non-parallel structure that does not grammatically connect to the rest of the sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — Landscape edited: places 'edited' immediately after the title with no punctuation, fusing both independent clauses without any separator. ✗
  • Choice D — Landscape. Edited: a period ends the first sentence cleanly; 'Edited by Barry Lopez and Debra Gwartney,' then works as an introductory participial phrase for the new sentence 'the book celebrates...' ✓
That is the correct answer.
